title: sanguisorba vulgaris subvar. magnolii
intro: Sanguisorba vulgaris subvar. magnolii, also known as Great Burnet, is a perennial plant that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae). Traditionally used in herbal medicine, Great Burnet is valued for its astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The medicinal properties of Great Burnet are believed to help reduce bleeding, soothe wounds, and alleviate digestive issues, offering various health benefits such as improved gut health, reduced inflammation, and enhanced wound healing. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of Great Burnet are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
